drizzt.name: Drizzt

canonical_lines.line: The gods know that I owe you more than that, my friend. Of course I'll train him.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt

canonical_lines.line: No more a slave than I.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt

canonical_lines.line: We are both indebted to the dwarf. I owe him my life many times over and thus have agreed to teach you my skill in battle. You follow an oath that you made to him in exchange for your life. Thus you are obliged to learn what I have to teach. I am no man's master, nor would I ever want to be.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt

canonical_lines.line: My people kill.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt

canonical_lines.line: This is vengeance. Let the giants see us, let them feel the terror of impending doom!
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt

tics: Complete, balanced sentences even under pressure
Moral framing and self-scrutiny arrive naturally in speech
Recasts hierarchy as debt, obligation, or chosen honor rather than domination
Threats stay calm and formal instead of slangy or boastful
avoid: Loose, jokey banter with no moral center

bruenor.name: Bruenor

canonical_lines.line: Get the notion outa yer head!
canonical_lines.speaker: Bruenor

canonical_lines.line: Yer mine 'til the last days of fall, don't ye forget that!
canonical_lines.speaker: Bruenor

canonical_lines.line: Ye've a spirit as wild as the tundra, ye've ne'er been, nor will ye ever be, a slave to any dwarf or man!
canonical_lines.speaker: Bruenor

canonical_lines.line: Get me a pig-squealin' giant to kill!
canonical_lines.speaker: Bruenor

canonical_lines.line: But don't ye never forget that ever ye'll be me boy!
canonical_lines.speaker: Bruenor

tics: Dwarf dialect drives the line: ye, yer, outa, suren, ne'er
Commands arrive as a bark, boast, or insult, but care leaks through
Paternal possessives like me boy and my girl carry emotional weight
Violence is phrased in blunt, bodily, practical terms

catti-brie.name: Catti-brie

canonical_lines.line: What drives you, I cannot tell. Luck to you on your adventure, but if you're taking it for no better reason than you have named, you're wasting your life:
canonical_lines.speaker: Catti-brie

canonical_lines.line: What indeed? Do you think that you hold it all in your oversized hands for no better reason than what you hold in your pants?
canonical_lines.speaker: Catti-brie

canonical_lines.line: Farewell, Wulfgar, son of Beornegar. When next we meet, I'll mark well your lessons of humility!
canonical_lines.speaker: Catti-brie

canonical_lines.line: I'll stay with the lever. You'll have enough to look after your own head up there. And someone has to be here to drop the tunnels; we cannot let goblins claim our halls as our home!
canonical_lines.speaker: Catti-brie

canonical_lines.line: Ye've truer friends than ever ye'll know, and not for the way ye twirl a sword. Ye've others who would call themselves friend if only they could get inside the length of yer arm - if only ye'd learn to look.
canonical_lines.speaker: Catti-brie

tics: Direct second-person challenge; she cuts through evasion fast
Warmth is braided with teasing, flirtation, or hard truth
Dwarf-inflected phrasing surfaces when she is emotional or emphatic
Speaks as a participant with agency, never as a protected bystander
avoid: Passive, observational dialogue that refuses commitment

entreri.name: Entreri

canonical_lines.line: You are a thief, or you are weak. Either way you shall find no mercy in my heart. Either way you deserve the wrath of Pook!
canonical_lines.speaker: Entreri

canonical_lines.line: I take no chances.
canonical_lines.speaker: Entreri

canonical_lines.line: Companion? Why would I desire a companion, little fool? What gain could I hope to make?
canonical_lines.speaker: Entreri

canonical_lines.line: I ask nothing of your business with them. Tell me only which one of the four it concerns.
canonical_lines.speaker: Entreri

canonical_lines.line: But I have the information I need. What do I gain in an alliance with you?
canonical_lines.speaker: Entreri

tics: Clipped, economical lines; questions are used like scalpels
Contempt is precise and calm, not loud
Frames people in terms of leverage, utility, and liability
Threats sound inevitable rather than theatrical

jarlaxle.name: Jarlaxle

canonical_lines.line: Where is the pirate, Pinochet?
canonical_lines.speaker: Pook
canonical_lines.note: No direct Jarlaxle dialogue exists in the Icewind Dale trilogy bundle; Pook's cultured, amused pressure is the closest in-bundle proxy for leverage-heavy theatrical banter.

canonical_lines.line: Ah, Entreri, ever you prove a headache, however well you serve me.
canonical_lines.speaker: Pook

canonical_lines.line: No, wait. Let me first have a cage built for it. Guenhwyvar will be mine in time. It will learn discipline.
canonical_lines.speaker: Pook

canonical_lines.line: But I have one more gift to give to you: a map to a treasure that you can recover this very day.
canonical_lines.speaker: Malchor
canonical_lines.note: Nearest-match proxy from Malchor's playful, performative guidance.

canonical_lines.line: A mask. An enchanted mask that will allow you to hide your heritage and walk freely as a surface elf - or as a man, if that suits you.
canonical_lines.speaker: Malchor

tics: Amused indirection; the line often performs while it pressures
Questions, gifts, and favors are used as leverage
Cultivated polish masks command intent
Charm can pivot into predation without changing the outward smoothness

zaknafein.name: Zaknafein

canonical_lines.line: My people kill.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt
canonical_lines.note: No direct Zaknafein dialogue exists in the Icewind Dale trilogy bundle; Drizzt's drow-ethos confession is the closest in-bundle proxy for compressed, disciplined drow severity.

canonical_lines.line: They kill without mercy.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt

canonical_lines.line: They kill without passion.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt

canonical_lines.line: No more a slave than I.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt
canonical_lines.note: Nearest-match proxy from Drizzt's teacher register.

canonical_lines.line: Perhaps it was the most important that I will ever teach.
canonical_lines.speaker: Drizzt
canonical_lines.note: Nearest-match proxy from Drizzt's teacher register.

tics: Compressed sentences with a drow-coded moral edge
Instruction and judgment arrive in the same breath
Discipline is valued over dominance or spectacle
Emotion is tightly contained until it hardens into severity
